items.original_body: Hello, I noticed that using tmux-popup breaks scroll in iTerm2. To get it back I have to switch on and off Preferences>Appearance>Windows>Hide scrollbars option. Not sure if this is fzf-tab issue or Iterm, but running nested tmux sessions from command line seems not messing up with scrolling.\r\n
items.mutated_body: Hello, I noticed that using the popup breaks scrolling in iTerm2. To get it back I have to toggle Preferences > Appearance > Windows > Hide scrollbars. Not sure if this is an fzf‑tab issue or iTerm, but running nested sessions from the command line does not mess up scrolling.

items.original_title: Alias block reference ID's with the source block
items.original_body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
When making block-level references, only the block ID is shown. This makes it difficult to make inline block references to specific parts.

![Screen Shot 2021-03-26 at 4 33 06 PM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/23715862/112702504-f393a700-8e50-11eb-87ec-416d45cd4c8a.png)


**Describe the solution you'd like**
 In other outliners like Roam and Remnote, the original block text is shown instead of the ID. Maybe it would be possible to create an alias for every block reference in a list such that the alias pulls from the original block text. 

For example:
![Screen Shot 2021-03-26 at 4 35 19 PM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/23715862/112702591-42d9d780-8e51-11eb-9eee-9bbef7d645e7.png)
becomes this in preview mode:
![Screen Shot 2021-03-26 at 4 35 38 PM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/23715862/112702605-4d946c80-8e51-11eb-951b-5069b6cba553.png)

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
I'm not sure about any alternatives to this.

items.original_title: Allow kubernetes_namespace resource to ignore Istio istio.io/rev label
items.original_body:
### Description
Currently, kubernetes_namespace resource is ignoring any labels ending with whose key names end with kubernetes.io because those can be mutated. When using Istio and particularly when upgrading Istio using [canary upgrades](https://istio.io/latest/docs/setup/upgrade/canary/), controlled during sidecar injection is based on the namespace label istio.io/rev and the value of this label is typically the new version of Istio being installed

example
istio.io/rev=1.9.0

then it keeps changing as the upgrades occur but terraform conflicts with the changing label value

can istio.io/rev label be ignored just like labels ending with "kubernetes.io" as both are affected by mutating values.

thanks
items.mutated_title: Allow kubernetes_namespace resource to ignore Istio istio.io/rev label

When using Istio and particularly when upgrading Istio using [canary upgrades](https://istio.io/latest/docs/setup/upgrade/canary/), controlled during sidecar injection is based on the namespace label istio.io/rev and the value of this label is typically the new version of Istio being installed example istio.io/rev=1.9.0 then it keeps changing as the upgrades occur but terraform conflicts with the changing label value can istio.io/rev label be ignored just like labels ending with "kubernetes.io" as both are affected by mutating values. thanks

items.original_title: How to make mapping for prompt?
items.original_body:
I am trying to make a mapping that like writing cd I'll get prompt to automatically fill in :cd that I can continue, but now instead of that it just does command cd.

`map cd :cd`
items.mutated_body: I am trying to make a mapping that like writing cd I'll get prompt to automatically fill in :cd that I can continue, but now instead of that it just does command cd. `map cd :cd`

items.original_title: message_reference.fail_if_not_exists is treated as if it should be a snowflake
items.original_body:
**Description**

The fail_if_not_exists parameter does not error as expected when not passing a boolean.

**Steps to Reproduce**

- Send a message with message_reference to a valid message_id.
- Set fail_if_not_exists in this message_reference to a string, such as 'test'
- Send the message again
- Set fail_if_not_exists to a snowflake string
- Send the message a third time

**Expected Behavior**

The API errors with: Value 'test' is not boolean.

**Current Behavior**

The API errors with Value 'test' is not snowflake. Further, if you send a snowflake, no error is hit and acts as if it were not sent at all, erroring if the message was deleted, or sending if it was not.

**Screenshots/Videos**

Sending with invalid string:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/8060149/107215086-b027cb00-69bf-11eb-9c79-53035cf981d5.png)
Sending with a snowflake:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/8060149/107215154-c9c91280-69bf-11eb-96bb-93f19cb329fc.png)

**Client and System Information**

Library: discord.js@master: PR branch https://github.com/discordjs/discord.js/pull/5298 commit hash: 
643f7d4e4cfdf316dbbefd9a8976589332cef6d6
Bot: Windows 10 Pro x64 v20H2, node v14.15..1
API version: 8

items.original_title: CLI crashes when the config file is missing
items.original_body: Running the command without a config file raises an uncaught exception instead of showing a helpful error.
